title: Best VPN for Sweden 2026: SVT Play, Privacy, and the FRA Law
description: The best VPNs for Sweden in 2026. Works with SVT Play and TV4 Play from abroad, Swedish privacy law explained, and Mullvad vs NordVPN vs ExpressVPN compared.
content:
Quick answer: NordVPN and ExpressVPN are the best VPNs for Sweden in 2026. Both have Swedish servers, work with SVT Play and TV4 Play from abroad, and have independently audited no-log policies. Sweden is a 14 Eyes country, so a no-log VPN adds meaningful privacy protection against ISP data retention.

Why Use a VPN in Sweden?

Sweden has one of the highest internet penetration rates in the world and a population that is broadly privacy-aware. Common reasons Swedes and visitors use VPNs include: accessing SVT Play, TV4 Play, and Viaplay from outside Sweden while traveling, securing connections on public Wi-Fi at airports and cafes, and protecting privacy from ISP data logging required under EU directives.

Sweden passed the FRA law in 2008, allowing the Swedish signals intelligence agency to monitor cross-border internet traffic without individual warrants. Sweden is also a member of the 14 Eyes intelligence alliance. For most ordinary users concerned about commercial data tracking rather than state surveillance, a no-log VPN provides solid protection against ISP data sharing and advertising profiles.

Best VPNs for Sweden

1. NordVPN

NordVPN operates servers in Stockholm and is one of the most consistent choices for accessing Swedish streaming content from abroad. Its no-log policy has been independently audited by PricewaterhouseCoopers and Deloitte. NordVPN also offers Meshnet (a feature for connecting devices directly via encrypted tunnel), which is useful for Swedish users accessing home networks while traveling. Two-year subscription plans bring the cost to competitive levels. NordVPN is headquartered in Panama, outside the 14 Eyes jurisdiction.

2. ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN has Swedish servers in Stockholm and performs well for SVT Play access from abroad. Its TrustedServer technology runs all servers on RAM, meaning no data is written to disk between sessions. ExpressVPN is slightly more expensive than NordVPN but has a consistent track record for maintaining streaming access even when platforms update geo-blocking. If you primarily need a VPN to watch SVT Play while traveling, ExpressVPN is a reliable choice. Based in the British Virgin Islands, outside 14 Eyes.

3. Mullvad VPN

Mullvad is a Swedish-founded VPN with strong privacy credentials and an unusual approach: it accepts anonymous payment (cash and cryptocurrency) and allows users to sign up without an email address. Mullvad has been independently audited and stores no usage logs. It is based in Gothenburg, Sweden, which some users view as a drawback given the FRA law, but Mullvad has specifically structured its operations so that no data exists to hand over. For privacy-focused Swedish users, Mullvad is worth considering alongside the international options.

Accessing Swedish Streaming from Abroad

The major Swedish streaming services with geo-restrictions are SVT Play (public broadcaster, requires Swedish IP), TV4 Play (commercial broadcaster, requires Swedish IP for some content), and Viaplay (subscription service, has international versions but Swedish-specific content requires Swedish IP).

To access these services from abroad: connect to a Swedish VPN server, clear your browser cookies and cache, then load the streaming site. If a specific server is detected and blocked, most VPN providers offer multiple Swedish server options to try. Both NordVPN and ExpressVPN have dedicated streaming servers that are updated when detection methods change.

VPN and Swedish Privacy Law

VPNs are legal in Sweden. GDPR applies to any VPN provider serving Swedish users, giving you rights over how your data is handled. A VPN does not make you immune to law enforcement requests if you are suspected of a crime, but for ordinary privacy from commercial data collection and ISP metadata logging, a reputable no-log VPN is effective.
